subHeadline: The engines Mercedes-Benz owners search for most, ranked by real 2025 enquiry volume — with our honest verdict on each.

verdict.label: Watch
verdict.text: — "Black Death" injector seal risk plus rear-chain complexity; buy with documented history. The defining diesel of a generation.

verdict.label: Safe
verdict.text: — oil cooler seal is the known weak point, but labour-heavy rather than catastrophic. The C/E/GLE/Sprinter workhorse.

verdict.label: Best
verdict.text: — the revised modern diesel; materially fewer failure reports than its OM651 predecessor.

verdict.label: Safe
verdict.text: — timing chain stretch on earlier examples; well-supported parts network across the MB platform.

verdict.label: Safe
verdict.text: — generally robust; occasional valve cover oil leaks. High-performance AMG V8 with an established specialist network.

verdict.label: Watch
verdict.text: — the earlier "Black Death" era; pre-2006 units particularly affected.

verdict.label: Best
verdict.text: — the legendary "will run forever" Mercedes diesel. The classic keeper engine.

legend.title: What do these verdicts mean?
legend.text: Our verdicts combine failure frequency, repair complexity, and total cost of ownership to help you make the right decision.
